documents.title: IU GenAI Task Force Final Report

ai_strategy.summary: IU has a systemwide AI at IU presence, a 2024 GenAI task force report, approved Microsoft Copilot access, and a GenAI 101 course auto-enrolled for students with staff/faculty opt-in. Bloomington is central to this systemwide work through Teaching.IU, faculty contributors, and the statewide university infrastructure.

key_provisions: Use AI in accordance with academic integrity expectations and instructor or unit guidance
Do not enter restricted, confidential, regulated, or sensitive data into unapproved AI tools
Review AI outputs for accuracy, hallucination, bias, copyright, and attribution issues
Disclose or cite AI use when required by course, research, publisher, or unit rules
Keep human judgment and accountability in consequential academic, research, and administrative decisions
ai_policy.summary: Indiana University Bloomington's AI policy posture is best characterized as comprehensive: public sources show explicit expectations for responsible use, privacy, disclosure, academic integrity, and human review, while leaving some implementation decisions to units or instructors.

bodies.name: IU Generative AI Task Force
bodies.scope: Systemwide faculty governance review of GenAI use and policy needs.

bodies.name: University Information Technology Services
bodies.scope: Maintains AI at IU, approved tools, and data-management guidance.

governance_structure.summary: Indiana University Bloomington's public AI governance is organized through IU Generative AI Task Force, University Information Technology Services.

initiatives.title: GenAI 101 systemwide course
initiatives.description: IU's GenAI 101 is a free, self-paced course for anyone with an IU login; all students are auto-enrolled, while faculty and staff can opt in.
initiatives.significance: Indiana University Bloomington's genai 101 systemwide course is a signal of how an R1 institution is adapting AI across policy, teaching, research, operations, or workforce needs.

investment.description: No specific dollar amount disclosed in consulted public sources.

collaborations.description: Course developed by a broad IU team.

initiatives.policy_implications: Supports responsible AI adoption through human oversight, privacy controls, disclosure, or course-level expectations.

tools_and_vendors.name: Crimson
tools_and_vendors.role: Conversational AI learning agent for GenAI 101 support.

initiatives.title: GenAI 101 badging and Crimson learning agent
initiatives.description: IU’s GenAI 101 auto-enrolled all students, offered free opt-in access for faculty and staff, later launched an alumni version for 805,000+ living alumni, and uses a Crimson conversational AI learning agent for on-demand course support.
initiatives.significance: This adds public usage scale and a named student-facing AI support component to IU’s AI-literacy strategy.

research_centers.name: IU Artificial Intelligence work through Luddy/Teaching.IU
research_centers.focus: AI teaching, data science, and computing activity across Bloomington and the IU system.

computing_infrastructure.summary: Indiana University operates Jetstream2 as NSF-funded national cyberinfrastructure for researchers and educators. Public IU sources describe Jetstream2 as an 8-petaflop cloud system with NVIDIA A100 GPUs and later AI Fellowship priority access to 90 GPU-enabled A100 nodes with H100 GPUs arriving; exact Bloomington-only GPU allocation is not published.

investments_and_gifts.title: NSF Jetstream2 award
investments_and_gifts.purpose: Create and operate Jetstream2, an IU-led cloud computing system for national research and education workloads including AI.

industry_and_research_partnerships.purpose: IU-approved Microsoft Copilot access with data protections.

student_facing_ai.name: GenAI 101
student_facing_ai.scope: All students are auto-enrolled in a practical generative AI skills course.

student_facing_ai.name: Crimson conversational AI learning agent
student_facing_ai.scope: On-demand support embedded in GenAI 101 for IU students enrolled in the self-paced course.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: Systemwide GenAI 101 access
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: Automatic student enrollment and faculty/staff opt-in broadens AI literacy access across disciplines.

regulatory_context.summary: Indiana University Bloomington operates in Indiana and within Indiana University; public AI guidance should be read alongside applicable state, federal, and system privacy, security, accessibility, and academic-integrity requirements.

narrative_summary: Indiana University Bloomington’s profile now distinguishes AI literacy scale from research-compute capacity. GenAI 101 auto-enrolled all IU students, gave faculty and staff free opt-in access, added a version for 805,000+ living alumni, and includes Crimson, a conversational AI learning agent for on-demand support. Research compute is anchored in IU-operated Jetstream2, an NSF-funded national system described as 8 petaflops with NVIDIA A100 GPUs and an AI Fellows program offering priority access to 90 GPU-enabled A100 nodes. Public gaps remain for Bloomington-only AI budgets, exact campus platform usage, exact H100/A100 allocation counts by campus, and subgroup outcome metrics.
